product_brief: A cloud key management service providing encryption, signing, and key encapsulation with software and HSM protection levels.
pqc_capability_description: PQC signing algorithms are in "Preview" status under Pre-GA Offerings Terms. Supported signing algorithms include ML-DSA-65 (Module-lattice-based) and SLH-DSA-SHA2-128S (Stateless hash-based), available as pure or pre-hash variants. Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs) are fully supported with ML-KEM-768, ML-KEM-1024 (FIPS 203), and KEM-XWING (hybrid).
crypto_primitives: AES-256-GCM, EdDSA (Curve25519), ECDSA (P-256, P-384, Secp256k1), RSA (PKCS#1 v1.5, RSASSA-PSS), HMAC (SHA-1 to SHA-512), ML-DSA, SLH-DSA, ML-KEM, X25519
key_management_model: Cloud-managed keys with protection levels for software or Hardware Security Module (HSM); supports Customer-Managed Encryption Keys (CMEK), External Key Manager (EKM) for off-cloud keys, and Autokey.
architecture_type: Managed cloud service with options for Single-tenant Cloud HSM and External Key Manager (EKM) integration.
license: Proprietary (subject to General Service Terms and Pre-GA Offerings Terms)
fips_validated: Yes (ML-KEM algorithms reference FIPS 203 parameter sets; specific module validation status not explicitly detailed beyond algorithm standards).
pqc_roadmap_details: PQC signing algorithms are currently in "Preview" (Pre-GA) status. Key Encapsulation Mechanisms include hybrid options like KEM-XWING for layered defense against classical and quantum adversaries.
signature_schemes: ECDSA, EdDSA, RSA-PSS, RSA-PKCS1, ML-DSA, SLH-DSA

product_brief: An AWS managed service to create and control keys used to encrypt or digitally sign data.
pqc_capability_description: The document does not mention Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC), PQC algorithms, or any plans for PQC migration. It only mentions standard symmetric encryption and ECDSA signing keys generated by the AWS Encryption SDK.
crypto_primitives: Symmetric key algorithm (unspecified specific algorithm), ECDSA (for signing in AWS Encryption SDK)
key_management_model: Cloud-managed service where keys are created, managed, used, and deleted entirely within AWS KMS; protected by FIPS 140-3 validated HSMs; supports customer-managed keys with key policies.
architecture_type: Managed cloud service backed by FIPS 140-3 validated hardware security modules (HSM)
license: Apache 2.0 (for AWS Encryption SDK); Commercial/Proprietary (for AWS KMS service)
latest_version: Not stated (mentions versions earlier than 1.7.x and versions 2.0.x for the SDK, but no specific latest version number)
fips_validated: Yes, FIPS 140-3 Security Level 3 validated hardware security modules (HSM)
primary_platforms: AWS Cloud; AWS Encryption SDK supports C, .NET, Java, JavaScript, Python, Rust, Go
signature_schemes: ECDSA (generated by AWS Encryption SDK for signing messages); Asymmetric KMS keys for signing operations

product_brief: A cloud service for securely storing and accessing secrets, including API keys, passwords, certificates, and cryptographic keys.
pqc_capability_description: Not stated in the provided text. The document mentions "Quantum computing" as a general Azure solution category but does not link it to Azure Key Vault's specific cryptographic capabilities or PQC algorithms.

key_management_model: Cloud-based managed service using Hardware Security Modules (HSMs); supports customer-managed keys (import from own HSMs) and provider-managed keys; centralized management of keys, secrets, and policies.
architecture_type: Managed cloud service (SaaS) utilizing HSMs for key storage and encryption.

target_industries: Enterprise, Small and Medium Businesses (mentioned in "Cloud solutions for small and medium businesses")
